# flatbuffers-rs

> Pure Rust FlatBuffers schema compiler. Drop-in replacement for C++ `flatc`
> with binary-compatible output. Compiles `.fbs` schemas to Rust, TypeScript,
> and binary schema (.bfbs) formats.

Source: https://github.com/Shuozeli/flatbuffers-rs

## What This Is

A from-scratch Rust implementation of the FlatBuffers schema compiler (`flatc`).
Takes `.fbs` schema files as input and produces the same generated code and
binary wire format as the official C++ `flatc`. Designed as a drop-in
replacement: same input, same output, same binary compatibility.

Use cases:
- Compile `.fbs` schemas to Rust or TypeScript without a C++ toolchain
- CI/CD schema evolution checking (`--conform`)
- Binary schema (.bfbs) generation for tooling
- JSON <-> FlatBuffers binary conversion

## Architecture

Nine crates in a Cargo workspace (plus a standalone grammar crate):

### schema

Schema type definitions mirroring the official FlatBuffers reflection schema
(`reflection.fbs`). Two layers: parsed types (all `Option<T>`, used during
parsing) and resolved types (`resolved.rs`, non-optional after analysis).
Also contains `BufReader` for safe binary buffer reads.

### parser

Hand-written recursive-descent parser. Tokenizes `.fbs` source text, then
parses into an unresolved Schema AST. Includes safety guards: 10MB input
limit and 10-second parse timeout.

### grammar (not a workspace member)

Tree-sitter grammar for the `.fbs` IDL. NOT used by the compilation pipeline.
NOT a workspace member (lives in `grammar/` but is not listed in workspace `Cargo.toml`).
Exists for IDE/editor integration (syntax highlighting, structural queries).

### codegen

Code generators for Rust and TypeScript:
- `rust_gen.rs` -- Rust code generation orchestrator
- `ts_gen.rs` -- TypeScript code generation orchestrator
- `enum_gen.rs` -- Rust enum and bitflags generation
- `ts_enum_gen.rs` -- TypeScript enum generation
- `struct_gen.rs` -- Rust struct generation
- `ts_struct_gen.rs` -- TypeScript struct generation
- `rust_table_gen/` -- Rust table reader, builder, Object API, and helpers
- `ts_table_gen/` -- TypeScript table reader, builder, Object API, and helpers
- `ts_type_map.rs` -- TypeScript type mapping
- `type_map.rs` -- Rust type mapping and name conversion
- `code_writer.rs` -- Shared indentation-aware code writer
- `namespace_tree.rs` -- Namespace hierarchy handling
- `service_gen.rs` -- gRPC service stub generation (feature-gated, `grpc`)

### compiler

The main orchestrator:
- `analyzer.rs` -- 8-step semantic analysis (type resolution, field ID assignment, struct layouts, etc.)
- `compiler.rs` -- Multi-file include resolution and schema merging
- `bfbs.rs` -- Binary schema (.bfbs) serialization and deserialization
- `conform.rs` -- Schema backwards-compatibility checking
- `error.rs` -- Error types (`AnalyzeError`, etc.)
- `struct_layout.rs` -- Struct byte layout computation (sizes, alignment, offsets)
- `type_index.rs` -- Type name resolution with parent namespace walking
- `reflection.rs` -- Generated FlatBuffers reflection types (from C++ flatc)
- `json/` -- JSON <-> FlatBuffers binary encoder/decoder
- `main.rs` -- CLI entry point (`flatc` binary)

### annotator

Binary annotation engine. Walks FlatBuffer binaries using schema metadata and
produces annotated output (`.afb` format matching C++ `flatc --annotate`).

### fbs-gen

Random valid `.fbs` schema generator for fuzz testing. Deterministic seeds.

### data-gen

Random JSON data generator from compiled schemas. For testing JSON round-trips.

### test-utils

Shared golden test framework. Tests are `.fbs` + `.expected` pairs.
`UPDATE_GOLDEN=1` env var regenerates expected files.

### wasm-api

WASM bindings (via wasm-bindgen) for browser-based compilation. Single-file only
(no `include` directive support). Exports 4 functions:
- `compile_fbs_to_rust(source, gen_object_api)` -- Schema to Rust code
- `compile_fbs_to_ts(source, gen_object_api)` -- Schema to TypeScript code
- `compile_fbs_to_bfbs(source)` -- Schema to binary schema bytes
- `annotate_flatbuffer(binary, schema_source, root_type_name)` -- Binary annotation

## CLI Usage

```bash
# Generate Rust code
flatc --rust -o out/ schema.fbs

# Generate Rust with Object API
flatc --rust --gen-object-api -o out/ schema.fbs

# Generate TypeScript
flatc --ts -o out/ schema.fbs

# Generate binary schema
flatc -b -o out/ schema.fbs

# JSON -> FlatBuffers binary
flatc -b schema.fbs -- data.json

# FlatBuffers binary -> JSON
flatc -t schema.fbs -- data.bin

# Schema evolution check
flatc --conform base_schema.fbs schema.fbs

# Include search paths
flatc --rust -I includes/ schema.fbs
```

## Supported Flags

Core: `-o`, `-I`, `--rust`, `--ts`, `-b`/`--schema`, `-t`/`--json`, `--root-type`,
`--gen-all`, `--gen-object-api`, `--gen-name-strings`, `--filename-suffix`,
`--filename-ext`, `--file-names-only`, `--no-warnings`, `--warnings-as-errors`

Rust-specific: `--rust-serialize`, `--no-leak-private-annotation`, `--no-includes`

TypeScript-specific: `--gen-mutable`

JSON/Binary: `--strict-json`, `--defaults-json`, `--force-defaults`,
`--unknown-json`, `--size-prefixed`

BFBS: `--bfbs-comments`, `--bfbs-filenames`, `--bfbs-absolute-paths`

Schema validation: `--conform`, `--conform-includes`

Binary inspection: `--annotate`, `--dump-schema`

## Build

```bash
# Build
cargo build --workspace

# Run all tests (612+ tests)
cargo test --workspace

# Regenerate golden files after intentional changes
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test --workspace
```

## Status

- 612+ tests passing
- Cross-compatibility verified against C++ flatc for monster_test, optional_scalars, more_defaults
- Binary-compatible output (same bytes as C++ flatc for same schema + data)
- Optional gRPC service generation (feature = "grpc")
- WASM compilation support for browser-based use

## Related Projects

- [fbsviewer-lib](https://github.com/Shuozeli/fbsviewer-lib) -- Interactive binary visualizer built on this compiler
- [fbsviewer.shuozeli.com](https://fbsviewer.shuozeli.com/) -- Live demo of the visualizer
